frameworks.name: 健康促進與協作流程
frameworks.desc: C01 Health and Wellness Promotion 與 C02 Integrative Design 要提供 WELL Feature Guide、health-oriented mission，並促進 collaborative planning process。

frameworks.name: 緊急準備與韌性資源
frameworks.desc: C03 Emergency Preparedness 與 C14 Emergency Resources 要求 risk assessment、emergency management plan、first aid kits、AED 與相關訓練資源。

frameworks.name: 使用者調查與回饋
frameworks.desc: C04 Occupant Survey 與 C05 Enhanced Occupant Survey 用第三方或 custom survey 評估 building users 的 experience、self-reported health and well-being。

frameworks.name: 包容、家庭與公共健康政策
frameworks.desc: C06 Health Services and Benefits、C08 New Parent Support、C09 New Mother Support、C10 Family Support、C12 Diversity and Inclusion 與 C13 Accessibility and Universal Design 將 health benefits、family policies、equity 與 universal design 納入社區健康。

questions.stem: According to Part 1: Facilitate Stakeholder Charrette of Feature C02: Integrative Design, when should a stakeholder charrette take place?

options.text: Just after the project has kicked off.
Continuously (monthly).
Early in the planning process.
After project completion.

questions.stem: According to Feature C02: Integrative Design Process, who must meet with stakeholders prior to the design and programming of a project trying to achieve a WELL Certification?

options.text: Engineers and business owner.
Project managers and business owner.
Project managers, business owner, architect.
Architects, owner, facility management team and engineers.

questions.stem: Part 2: Promote Health-Oriented Mission of Feature C02: Integrative Design stipulates that the project establishes a health-oriented mission. Where should the health-oriented mission be detailed?

options.text: A general notice to all occupants.
WELL Feature Guide
Project Checklist
Project Matrix

questions.stem: The WELL Building Standard™ (WELL™) Community concept seeks to establish an inclusive, engaged occupant community. What factors link people within a community?

options.text: Social ties.
Shared common perspectives.
Engaging in joint action and experiences in shared settings or locations.
All Of The Above.

questions.source_explanation: Read the overview for the Community Concept.

questions.stem: What are the Social Determinants of Health?

options.text: Environmental
Physical and built conditions
Mental
All of the Above

questions.source_explanation: Read the overview for the Community concept.

questions.stem: Which of the following is not a health disparity, according to the WELL Community concept?

options.text: Gender
Race
Ethnicity
Age

questions.source_explanation: Read the overview for the Community concept.

questions.stem: The U.S. Federal Emergency Management Agency estimates what percent of small businesses permanently close following a disaster due to the lack of a comprehensive disaster preparedness plan with proper mitigation strategies?
questions.source_explanation: Read overview of Feature C03: Emergency Preparedness in the Community concept.

questions.stem: How does Part 1: Select Project Survey of Feature C04: Occupant Survey require a project team to collect feedback from building users?

options.text: A questionnaire given to the largest building tenant.
Third-party survey from approved IWBI vendor.
A survey sent out via email.
A suggestion drop box.

questions.stem: What can an employer do to support the overall health and well-being of individuals and their families according to Part 1: Promote Health Benefits of Feature C06: Health Services and Benefits?

options.text: Allow employees flexible working hours or the opportunity to work remotely a few days a week.
Purchase fitbits or other fitness trackers for those wanting to get healthier.
Offer comprehensive health benefits, policies and services.
Run a marathon with a company team.

questions.stem: Which of the following does a property management team need to do when conducting a post-occupancy survey, as stipulated in Part 2: Utilize Pre- and Post-Occupancy Survey of Feature C05: Enhanced Occupant Survey?

options.text: Use a third party created survey.
Conduct the survey themselves in the building.
Ensure those taking the survey are not forced to do so.
Compare results from the pre-occupancy survey against post-occupancy survey results..

questions.stem: The WELL overview for Feature C06: Health Services and Benefits tells us that 94% of the world's countries mandate paid sick leave. The United States and this country are the only OECD countries that do not mandate paid sick leave.

options.text: Japan
Korea
Turkey
Chile

questions.stem: According to Feature C06: Health Services and Benefits, 94% of the world’s countries mandate paid sick leave, the U.S. and Korea are the only OECD countries that do not, and 40% of American employees have no sick leave. Studies estimate that how many Americans go to work sick because they lack sick leave or have only one-day of sick leave?
questions.source_explanation:
Studies also show that implementing paid sick leave reduces contagion in the 
workplace, improves employee productivity and reduces employee turnover. Overall, 
enhancing access to essential healthcare and paid sick leave can help improve the 
physical, social and mental health of individuals and communities.

questions.stem: Studies show that implementing _______ reduces contagion in the workplace and improves employee productivity and reduces employee turnover.

options.text: Employer-paid telemedicine visits.
Paid sick leave.
Open concept floor plans.
Healthy lunches paid for by the employer daily.

questions.source_explanation: Feature C06: Health Services and Benefits.

questions.stem: Successful health promotion programs can improve job satisfaction, according to Feature C07: Enhanced Health and Wellness Promotion. Specifically, medical and absenteeism costs fall by about $3.27 and $2.73, respectively, for every dollar spent on _______.

options.text: Quarterly company retreats.
Workplace health programs.
Office refreshments.
Company matched 401(k).

questions.source_explanation: Read overview for Feature C07: Enhanced Health and Wellness Promotion.

questions.stem: A tech corporation is pursuing WELL Certification for their new corporate headquarters in Austin, Texas. In order to receive 1 Point for Part 2: Offer On-Demand Health Services of Feature: C06 Health Services and Benefits a WELL Certified building must provide _______ ?

options.text: Cafeteria Head Chef
Onsight Doctor or Nurse Practitioner
Chief Wellness Officer
Physical Trainer

questions.stem: Part 1: Offer New Parent Leave of Feature C08: New Parent Support requires paid leave to be at ____ or higher of the employee's full salary or wages and cover benefits.
At a minimum, WELL Feature C08: New Parent Support promotes how much paid leave for the primary caregiver and non-primary caregiver?

options.text: Primary Caregiver at least 2 weeks | Non-Primary Caregiver 5 days.
Primary Caregiver at least 4 Weeks | Non-Primary At least 2 Weeks.
Primary Caregiver at least 12 Weeks | Non-Primary 2 Weeks.
Primary Caregiver at least 30 Weeks | Non-Primary 4 Weeks.

questions.stem: Parental support policies help employees and their families in a variety of ways. Higher rates of breastfeeding and reduced infant mortality are some of the benefits of paid parental leave. Which of the following is not a policy that a project can implement to be WELL compliant?

options.text: Coaching programs, counseling or other resources to help employees transition when returning from leave.
Use of virtual assistance to help cover the work load.
Training for managers to help create a plan for leave and optimal support for returning to work.
Communication to expecting parents about the project or organization’s parental leave policies and supporting resource.

questions.source_explanation: Read overview for Feature C08: New Parent Support.

questions.stem: Part 1: Offer Workplace Breastfeeding Support of Feature C09: New Mother Support provides spaces and policies that encourage and support breastfeeding. How many pumping sessions per eight-hour workday should be allocated for an eligible employee?
questions.source_explanation:
Paid break times for pumping, at least 15-20 minutes every 2-3 hours (or 2-3 
pumping sessions per eight-hour workday), with adjustments as necessary to meet 
the needs of individuals.

questions.stem: A breastfeeding mother is traveling for work. She and her team will be visiting clients and their trip should only last 1 full day and half of the following day (36 hours). What travel accommodations should be made according to Part 1: Offer Workplace Breastfeeding Support of Feature C09: New Mother Support?

options.text: A hotel room or other accommodations with refrigerator access.
An insulated cooler purchased by the employee.
Coverage of express shipping of milk to family back home.
Reschedule return flight home to an earlier flight.

questions.stem: The intent of Feature C10: Family Support is the support working parents and caregivers so that they are able to properly care for members of their families. If a project doesn't have an on-site childcare center, what percentage of off-site or at-home childcare costs should be subsidized for employees?
questions.source_explanation: Found in Part 1:  Offer Childcare Support of Feature C10: Family Support.

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT included in offering childcare as part of Feature C10: Family Support?

options.text: On-site childcare centers compliant with local childcare licensure, or subsidies of at least 50% for off-site or at-home childcare, for regular occupants.

Back-up childcare coverage for regular occupants in case of unexpected events, at no cost or subsidized by at least 50% (e.g., drop-in daycare, overnight childcare, in-home babysitting service, virtual childcare service).
Seasonal childcare programs (e.g., center- or home-based care during school break or winter holidays) for regular occupants with school-age children.
Employees are able to bring their children to the office on days their kids are sick.

questions.stem: An employee needs to take time off to care for their spouse who was diagnosed with cancer. At the very least, how many weeks of paid leave should this employee receive?

options.text: 2 weeks of paid leave paid at 50% or higher of employees full salary or wages.
4 weeks of paid leave paid at 70% or higher of employees full salary or wages.
12 weeks of paid leave paid at 75% or higher of employees full salary or wages.
16 weeks of paid leave paid at 75% or higher of employees full salary or wages.

questions.source_explanation: Part 2: Offer Family Leave of Feature C10: Family Support.

questions.stem: The WELL Building Standard offers family leave in Feature C10: Family Support. When an employee hits the 12 week threshold when receiving 75% pay for being out taking care of a terminal spouse, all of the following with the exception of ____ are options given to employees.

options.text: Forced to return or resign.
Use paid sick time or personal days for the care of a spouse.
Arrive late or leave early daily.
Permanently work from home.

questions.stem: Acme Corp is very passionate about its community. Every year they host and are involved in various events to give back and show the community they care. Which of the following are examples of community engagement as it pertains to WELL?

options.text: A yearly 5k race and cookout that supports charity
Donation matching charity drives to organization of employee's choosing
Halloween Costume Contest
March Madness Bracket and Prize Pool
